item.title: [Google Ads] Fix THIS & Get Better Results with Demand Gen in Google Ads
item.description:
These are the 3 biggest mistakes that we see time & time again with Demand Gen campaigns in Google Ads.
In this video I am going to take you through these mistakes and then also show you a live example of how you can fix these issues.
👉 Take Control of Your Own Business & Learn to Use Google Ads

item.title: [General] Dominate Local SEO: Service Area Pages That Rank #shorts
item.description: Showcase your market expertise with dedicated pages for each service and neighborhood. Highlight local projects with photos and videos. Like Orkin, target geographic keywords to rank higher for local searches like 'pest control Charleston SC'. #LocalSEO #DigitalMarketing #ServicePages #KeywordReleva

item.title: [Meta Ads] Does Adding More Ads Actually Hurt Performance?
item.description: Today's question is about whether adding too many ads to a single ad set can actually hurt delivery. Meta removed its old recommendation of no more than six ads per ad set, but that doesn't mean more is always better. Jon explains why starting small with a couple of creatively diverse ads and adding

item.title: [General] Dominate Local SEO: The Ultimate Review & Citation Strategy #shorts
item.description: Master local SEO by perfecting your Google Business Profile. Ensure NAP consistency, build a repeatable review engine, and create dedicated service pages. Drive rankings with citations and local mentions. #LocalSEO #GoogleBusinessProfile #DigitalMarketing #SmallBusiness

item.title: [Google Ads] Turn your data into decisions: 3 things your business needs for growth in the AI era
item.description: To move at the speed of AI, simplify your data and understand what’s driving growth. Learn more in the lead-up to Google Marketing Live.

item.title: [General] What I learned when making the jump from salaried to self-employed PPC Manager
item.description: Like many freelancers, my origin story stemmed from seeking the elusive “work-life balance” we dream of after having a family, but before then I was a Senior PPCer at a North East UK digital agency. I had grown the PPC team from 3 staff to 10 over the space of 4 years, and I was […]

item.description: Google’s latest guidance on AI and search has a simple surface message and a more important operational one underneath it. The surface message is familiar: keep creating useful content, keep serving users, and do not assume AI changes the need for quality. The deeper message is that search behavi

item.title: [General] Google’s Search Engineer Explains “Black Box” AI Models: What It Actually Means for AI Overviews, AI Mode, and SEO
item.description: Google’s use of AI in Search is often discussed in broad, abstract terms. The language tends to swing between two extremes. On one side, AI is described as a magic layer changing everything overnight. On the other, it is framed as a threat to the open web, with little nuance about how it actually

item.title: [General] Google March 2026 Core Update Analysis: Sites that Gained, Lost and What Changed in Search
item.description: Google’s March 2026 core update was not a routine reshuffle. It was one of those updates where the patterns became visible quickly enough that even cautious observers could see a broad directional change: visibility moved toward primary sources, recognized brands, official institutions, and speci

item.title: [General] Google Says Websites Must Work for AI Agents Too: A Practical Guide for Developers, SEO Teams, and Site Owners
item.description: The web is being used in a new way. For years, site owners designed pages for two audiences: human visitors and search crawlers. Now there is a third audience that matters in a very direct way: AI agents that can interpret pages, plan actions, click through workflows, compare options, fill forms,

item.title: [General] Microsoft Says Bing Reached 1 Billion Monthly Active Users: What It Means for Search, SEO, and Advertising
item.description: Microsoft says Bing has reached 1 billion monthly active users for the first time. On its own, that is a notable platform milestone. In context, it is even more interesting. The same update also included several signals that matter to marketers, publishers, advertisers, and business leaders: sear

Starting in June 2026, granular Google Ads reporting data will no longer remain available indefinitely inside the platform’s

item.title: [General] Dollar General’s New Onsite and Offsite Retail Media Solution: What It Means for Brands, Agencies, and the Future of Commerce Media

Until now, brands often had to manage onsite retail media and offsite media through separate systems, separate workflows, and se

That is where advice gets shared, stress gets diffused, small emergencies get solved, and practical help ge

However, it’s nearly impossible to fake with AI…

item.title: [General] What is your experience with AI Max?
item.description:
Full Disclosure… I really like AI Max and I want to use it more (as I see it has lots of potential) BUT for me it’s not quite ready yet.
Here’s why 👇
As part of an account audit I did last week, I found AI Max had been accidentally switched on for 5 weeks, and the results were:
→ 83% of the searc

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Business AI Reaches 10 Million Weekly Conversations: What It Means for WhatsApp, Messenger, Advertisers, and Growing Businesses
item.description:

The

item.title: [General] Google Added 25 Million Paid Subscriptions in Q1: What YouTube, Google One, and Gemini Say About Alphabet’s Next Phase
item.description: Google added 25 million paid subscriptions in the first quarter, lifting its total to 350 million. On the surface, that is a straightforward earnings headline. In practice, it says much more. It suggests Alphabet is becoming less dependent on a single monetization model, more disciplined about bu

item.title: [General] Viral Marketing Campaigns in 2026: What Works, Why It Spreads, and How Brands Turn Attention Into Revenue
item.description: Viral marketing still gets misunderstood. People talk about it as if it were a lucky break, a meme, or a single post that suddenly explodes for no clear reason. That version exists, but it is not the version serious brands should build around in 2026. The campaigns that spread now are rarely rand

item.title: [General] Microsoft Copilot Reaches 20 Million Paid Users: What Enterprise Adoption and Usage Data Really Show

item.title: [General] AI SEO Best Practices for Real Estate Agents: A Practical Guide to Local Visibility, Google AI Overviews, and Better Leads
item.description: Most real estate SEO advice still assumes search works the way it did a few years ago. It does not. Buyers and sellers now search across Google, Google Maps, YouTube, Reddit, brokerage sites, listing portals, and AI assistants. They type shorter queries, ask longer questions, compare neighborhood

item.title: [General] Instagram Restricts Reach for Content Aggregators: What the New Originality Rules Mean for Creators, Brands, and Publishers
item.description: Instagram’s latest originality update is straightforward in intent but broad in impact. Accounts that mostly repost photos and carousels they did not create, without adding meaningful creative value, are no longer eligible for recommendation across the platform. That matters because recommendatio

item.title: [General] Spotify Verified Artist Badge: What “Verified by Spotify” Means for Human Artists, AI Music, and Listeners

item.title: [General] Salesforce Is Letting Customers Shape Its AI Roadmap: What That Means for Agentforce, Enterprise AI Strategy, and CRM Buyers

item.description: The introduction of the new creative workflow coincides with the elimination of Flexible Format. Advertisers won't miss it. Read more about this update at jonloomer.com/workflow.

It came from a TikTok account posting short episodes about anthropomorphic fruit in a dating-show villa. That is the simple version of the story. The more imp

item.description: Built for the next generation of Search, AI Max for Shopping campaigns helps retailers reach shoppers the moment discovery begins.

item.title: [Google Ads] Meet travelers in the moments that matter with Search Campaigns for Travel.
item.description: Upgrade to AI Max to simplify your workflow and access our most advanced tools.

item.title: [Google Ads] AI Max Turns 1 with new ways to steer performance and expansion to more advertisers
item.description: As AI Max turns 1, we’re helping you capture even more opportunities in the expanding Search universe.

item.title: [General] 縦型動画広告の成果につながる冒頭設計とは？BtoC・BtoBの添削事例から学ぶ改善のヒント
item.description:
「なんとなくイケてない気がするけど、どこをどう直せばいいか分からない」 縦型動画広告の制作で、一度はこう感じたことはないでしょうか。成果の差が最も出やすいのが、動画の冒頭数秒の設計です。 本セミナーで…

The post 縦型動画広告の成果につながる冒頭設計とは？BtoC・BtoBの添削事例から学ぶ改善のヒント first appeared on アナグラム株式会社.
item.category: アナグラム ブログ

item.title: [Meta Ads] Meta Ads AI Connectors, App Usage Drops, and More
[General] Adobe Completes Semrush Acquisition: What It Means for SEO, GEO, AI Search Visibility, and Brand Discovery
item.description: Adobe has completed its acquisition of Semrush, closing a deal that moves one of the best-known names in search intelligence into one of the largest customer experience and content ecosystems in enterprise software. On the surface, this looks like a classic platform acquisition: Adobe adds anothe

item.title: [General] Nick Jonas Joins Schick’s “Do Right By Your Skin” Campaign
item.description: If a lot of people briefly thought Nick Jonas was about to launch a skincare line, that was the point. Schick’s “Do Right By Your Skin” campaign arrived with a simple but carefully staged idea: get consumers to look at a razor the way they look at skincare. Instead of talking about shaving as a [

item.title: [General] The 3-Layer Content Framework: How to Align Brand Truth, Audience Relevance, and Channel Fit
item.description: Every strong piece of content does three jobs at once. It represents the company accurately. It matters to the audience immediately. And it matches the environment where people will encounter it. Most content misses at least one of those jobs. Some posts are on-brand but generic. They sound polis

item.title: [General] Why Channel-by-Channel Marketing Is Dying: What the Data Shows and What to Do Instead
item.description: There was a time when channel-by-channel marketing felt sensible. You had a search team, an email team, a social team, maybe a paid media team and a web team. Each group had its own calendar, budget, reports, and goals. If performance looked healthy inside each channel dashboard, leadership assum

item.title: [General] From Bot to Buyer: How Conversational AI Turns Website Conversations Into Qualified Sales 24/7
item.description: A missed lead rarely announces itself. It shows up as a visitor who lands on your pricing page after business hours, asks one or two important questions, gets no useful answer, and leaves. It looks like a buyer who is ready to book a demo but meets a generic form instead of a timely conversation.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Retires 4 Legacy Ad Policies: What the Streamline Means for Your Campaigns
item.description: Google has discontinued several legacy ad format policies, and while this is not the kind of change that should trigger panic in an active Google Ads account, it is the kind of change that smart advertisers should pay attention to. On the surface, the update looks administrative. Google removed p

item.title: [General] True Value’s Official Sponsor of Grass Campaign: Full Breakdown of the Spring Strategy, SB Mowing Partnership, and Retail Impact
item.description: Every spring, lawn care advertising starts to look familiar. Retailers talk about greener grass, stronger roots, better fertilizer, faster weed control, and the tools homeowners need to get the yard back in shape after winter. The products matter, of course, but the marketing often blends togethe

item.title: [General] YouTube TV Multiview Is Rolling Out to More Subscribers: What Changed, How It Means, and How to Use Custom Multiview
item.description: YouTube TV’s multiview feature has moved from being a useful but limited option into something much closer to what subscribers had been asking for since the feature first appeared. More users are now getting access to a broader multiview experience, and the change is not just about seeing multipl

item.title: [General] Vine Is Back as diVine: What the Comeback Means, How the App Works, and Whether 6-Second Video Still Fits Today
item.description: Vine is back, but not in the way many people assume. The original Vine app is not being restored by X, and this is not simply a nostalgia project that replays a few old clips for longtime internet users. What has returned is a new app called diVine, a six-second looping video platform built to re

item.title: [General] Meta Q1 2026: Daily Active People Fell to 3.56 Billion While Revenue Reached $56.31 Billion
item.description: For years, Meta’s scale story has seemed almost untouchable. The company’s family of apps has operated with such reach, such habitual use, and such global depth that even small fluctuations in user activity attracted outsized attention. That is why Meta’s first-quarter 2026 report stood out immed
